req.title: Cheque deposit by mobile (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
preconditions: authenticated

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a mobile cheque deposit flow with: (1) a happy path where a valid cheque under the $5,000 threshold is accepted and queued, (2) a manual-verification branch for amounts above $5,000, (3) error handling for MICR decode failures (422), and (4) negative paths for insufficient funds, unauthorised access, and exceeded limits. Equivalence Partitioning covers the under-limit valid deposit. Boundary Value Analysis targets $5,000 exactly and $5,001. Negative Path covers MICR failure, unauthorised, and exceeded-limit scenarios. Decision Table maps the combination of amount vs. verification flag. State Transition Coverage confirms the deposit moves from PENDING to QUEUED_FOR_MANUAL_REVIEW when above the threshold.

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_valid_under_limit
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Cheque image passes MICR decode
Deposit amount is $1,000 (well within the $5,000 limit)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload and amount=1000
trace: R-FIN-069: valid mobile cheque deposit accepted and queued
R-FIN-069: cheques <= $5,000 do not require manual verification
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_valid_under_limit(auth_headers, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 1000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING"
    assert body["manual_review"] is False
    assert "deposit_id" in body

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_micr_decode_failure_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Cheque image is corrupted or unreadable by the MICR parser
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with a corrupt image payload
Assert error code == 'MICR_DECODE_FAILURE' in response body
trace: R-FIN-069: error pathway - 422 when image fails MICR decode
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_micr_decode_failure_returns_422(auth_headers, corrupt_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": corrupt_cheque_image,
        "amount": 500,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error_code"] == "MICR_DECODE_FAILURE"
    assert "deposit_id" not in body

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_exact_limit_requires_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Cheque image passes MICR decode
Deposit amount is exactly $5,000 (the boundary value)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload and amount=5000
trace: R-FIN-069: cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification
R-FIN-069: boundary at exactly $5,000 triggers manual review
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_exact_limit_requires_manual_review(auth_headers, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING"
    assert body["manual_review"] is True
    assert "deposit_id" in body

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_one_cent_above_limit_requires_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Cheque image passes MICR decode
Deposit amount is $5,000.01 (one cent above the boundary)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload and amount=5000.01
trace: R-FIN-069: cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification
R-FIN-069: one cent above boundary must also trigger manual review
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_one_cent_above_limit_requires_manual_review(auth_headers, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 5000.01,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING"
    assert body["manual_review"] is True

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: No authentication token is provided
Cheque image is valid
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload but no Authorization header
trace: R-FIN-069: precondition - authenticated; unauthenticated requests must be rejected

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_exceeded_daily_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer has already reached their daily mobile deposit limit
Cheque image passes MICR decode
actions: Seed the customer account to have exhausted the daily deposit limit via <seed_daily_limit_exhausted>
POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image payload and amount=100
trace: R-FIN-069: decision table - valid image AND over limit => 422
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_exceeded_daily_limit_returns_422(auth_headers, valid_cheque_image):
    <seed_daily_limit_exhausted>(auth_headers)
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error_code"] == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    assert "deposit_id" not in body

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_above_limit_state_transitions_to_manual_queue
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Cheque image passes MICR decode
Deposit amount is $7,500 (above $5,000 threshold)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image and amount=7500
Assert manual_review == true and decisioning_queue_ref is present
expected: decisioning_queue_ref is non-null, confirming queue entry
trace: R-FIN-069: cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_above_limit_state_transitions_to_manual_queue(auth_headers, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
        "amount": 7500,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    create_resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 202
    deposit_id = create_resp.json()["deposit_id"]
    get_resp = <api_client>.get(f"/deposits/{deposit_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING"
    assert body["manual_review"] is True
    assert body.get("decisioning_queue_ref") is not None
